summary: Frontend-only retest of the SaxophoneScene loading-indicator fix. All 5 acceptance criteria confirmed: (1) Loader (drei useProgress) renders correctly while the GLB is downloading — observed under throttled network as a small gold spinner + uppercase tracked text 'SUMMONING THE ICON' centered in the hero canvas area, with sparkles already animating around it. Spinner DOM element (.animate-spin) present (count=1). (2) After the GLB finishes loading (in unthrottled run, the cached preloaded 1.6MB /models/saxophone.glb resolved within ~800ms; in throttled run it appears after the download completes), the large GOLDEN SAXOPHONE is clearly visible in the hero, occupying roughly the right half of the viewport, with shimmer highlights, deep shadows, sparkles around it, and the headline 'Discover future icons before everyone else' overlapping it. WebGL pixel sample on 1920x800 canvas: nonZeroPct=7.60%, goldPct=5.06%, brightPct=5.21% — matches/exceeds the previous good reference. (3) Once progress>=100 the 'Summoning the icon' loader disappears (count=0 at 20s on unthrottled run). (4) ZERO uncaught page errors and ZERO console errors on the landing page (after filtering benign posthog / THREE.Clock / readPixels noise per spec). (5) Regression: /discover loads the artist grid (13 artist-* testid elements, screenshot shows Nova Ashe, Vela, Cipher, Riven cards rendered); nav-home / nav-discover / nav-vault / nav-pulse all present (count=1 each); returning to '/' re-mounts data-testid='saxophone-scene'. Loader fix verified end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: SaxophoneScene.jsx Loader3D: gating on `progress >= 100` from drei useProgress works, but useProgress is global to drei's asset registry — if another GLTF/asset starts loading later (e.g. on route change), progress could drop below 100 and the loader would re-show over the hero. Currently no other GLBs are loaded on landing so it's fine, but worth tracking only this specific model's progress (via onProgress in useGLTF) if more 3D assets are added.

useGLTF.preload(MODEL_URL) at module top-level means the GLB starts downloading as soon as the SaxophoneScene module is imported (i.e. at app boot), even on routes that don't render it. For a 1.6MB asset this is a deliberate UX tradeoff (warm cache for landing) — acceptable, but worth re-evaluating if the landing route is no longer the default entry.
Loader3D markup uses absolute inset-0 inside a pointer-events-none wrapper — verified the spinner does not block the 'Become a Spotter' CTA underneath. Good.
No data-testid on the loader itself; consider data-testid='saxophone-loader' to make future regression tests target it directly instead of relying on the visible text. Minor / nice-to-have.
test_credentials: Not exercised this iteration (no auth flows in the requested scope). Seeded admin per spec: admin@artistvault.com / vault123.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Loader behaviour is gated by drei useProgress (global). On an unthrottled run the preloaded 1.6MB GLB resolves in <1s so the loader window is essentially invisible — to verify the loader still works, throttle the network via CDP Network.emulateNetworkConditions (downloadThroughput ~60KB/s) with cacheDisabled=true; the spinner + 'SUMMONING THE ICON' text becomes visible centered in the hero within ~700ms (see .screenshots/sax_loader.png). To verify the loaded saxophone, use viewport 1920x800 and sample canvas pixels via gl.readPixels — expect goldPct > 2% and brightPct > 3%. Saxophone-scene wrapper testid: 'saxophone-scene'.
rca of the issue: Original symptom (per main agent context): hero looked empty/broken for the ~12-16s it took the proxied 1.6MB GLB to download+parse, with no visual indication of loading. Fix: Loader3D component added in SaxophoneScene.jsx using drei useProgress — renders a small gold spinner + 'Summoning the icon' label inside the hero canvas wrapper until progress>=100, then unmounts itself. Verified visually under throttled network (loader present) and at full speed (loader transitions cleanly to the rendered gold saxophone). No regressions in hero composition, /discover roster, or top-nav.
